questions.stem: These liver histology panels use a special histochemical stain that turns accumulated intracellular deposits bright blue. Which stain produces this reaction?

explanation.summary: The Prussian blue (Perls) reaction stains ferric iron in hemosiderin bright blue, demonstrating iron overload within hepatocytes and Kupffer cells in hemochromatosis.
explanation.whyCorrect: The Prussian blue (Perls) reaction stains ferric iron in hemosiderin bright blue, demonstrating iron overload within hepatocytes and Kupffer cells in hemochromatosis.

questions.stem: This slit-lamp photograph shows a brownish ring at the corneal margin of the eye. What substance accumulates, and in which layer, to produce this finding?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Copper deposited in the Descemet membrane of the cornea

choices.label: C
choices.text: Cholesterol deposited in the peripheral cornea

choices.label: D
choices.text: Melanin accumulation in the iris stroma

choices.label: E
choices.text: Iron deposited in the corneal epithelium

explanation.summary: The pigmented ring is a Kayser-Fleischer ring, caused by copper depositing in the Descemet membrane of the cornea in Wilson disease due to impaired biliary copper excretion.
explanation.whyCorrect: The pigmented ring is a Kayser-Fleischer ring, caused by copper depositing in the Descemet membrane of the cornea in Wilson disease due to impaired biliary copper excretion.

questions.stem: This liver biopsy shows round pink globules accumulating within hepatocytes in the disorder indicated. What is the characteristic histochemical staining property of these globules?

explanation.summary: In alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency, misfolded A1AT accumulates in the hepatocyte endoplasmic reticulum as globules that are PAS-positive and resistant to diastase digestion.
explanation.whyCorrect: In alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency, misfolded A1AT accumulates in the hepatocyte endoplasmic reticulum as globules that are PAS-positive and resistant to diastase digestion.
